channel.title: NYT > Technology
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Why So Many People Already Own Shares of Elon Musk’s SpaceX
item.description: Even before the rocket company holds a major initial public offering, many people own stock in it through so-called special purpose vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Erin Griffith and Ryan Mac
item.media:credit: Brandon Celi

item.title: Pentagon Makes Deals With A.I. Companies to Expand Classified Work
item.description: The agreements with the technology companies come amid the Defense Department’s dispute with Anthropic.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Sheera Frenkel
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Anthropic and the Pentagon have been locked in a debate over whether the company’s A.I. model could be used to pilot autonomous drones or work on domestic surveillance.

item.title: Elon Musk’s A.I. Claims of Danger Face Limits in OpenAI Trial
item.description: One of Elon Musk’s abiding fears is that A.I. could one day threaten humans. But the jurors deciding his suit against OpenAI probably won’t hear about it.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac
item.media:credit: Manuel Orbegozo/Reuters
item.media:description: Elon Musk returned to the witness stand in federal court for a third day on Thursday in what is expected to be a monthlong trial.

item.title: Struggling With Phone Addiction? Try These Remedies.
item.description: Counting minutes is a poor way to curb phone habits. Real antidotes, experts say, include mindful parenting, curated content and human connection.
item.dc:creator: Brian X. Chen
item.media:credit: Derek Abella

item.title: Jeeves and Ask.com Shut Down After Almost 30 Years
item.description: The pioneering search engine shut down on May 1, after nearly 30 years in operation.
item.dc:creator: Ali Watkins
item.media:credit: Newscast/Universal Images Group, via Getty Images
item.media:description: The Ask.com search engine website, with the illustration of the character Jeeves.

item.title: Driving Electric in Costa Rica Is Surprisingly Doable
item.description: The charging network is spotty, but it’s a small country.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing
item.media:credit: Tony Cenicola/The New York Times
item.media:description: Costa Rica requires utilities to install fast-charging stations every 50 miles on national highways. But many chargers don’t work.

item.title: These Countries Embrace E.V.s to Avoid Oil Price Shocks
item.description: People in Costa Rica and other Latin American, Asian and African countries are increasingly buying electric vehicles to avoid spiking fuel prices.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing and Tony Cenicola
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Guangzhou Xiaopeng Motors Technology Co Ltd (Xpeng Motors)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:credit: Tony Cenicola/The New York Times

item.title: How Running Shoes Have Evolved, From Ancient Greece to a Record-Breaking Marathon Time
item.description: The race to near-weightlessness has been a driving force of innovation in running sneakers and helped lead to records shattering at the London Marathon.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan
item.media:credit: Justin Tallis/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: OpenAI’s Big Reset + A.I. in the Doctor’s Office + Talkie, a pre-1930s LLM
item.description: Will the rising tide of A.I. adoption lift all boats?
item.dc:creator: Kevin Roose, Casey Newton, Whitney Jones, Rachel Cohn, Vjeran Pavic, Daniel Ramirez, Dan Powell, Marion Lozano, Rowan Niemisto and Diane Wong
item.media:credit: Photo Illustration by The New York Times; Photo: Getty Images

item.title: Musk vs. Altman: What Is This Really About?
item.description: In a landmark trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI’s Sam Altman, the origins of OpenAI are being examined. The Times’s technology reporter Cade Metz explains what’s behind it all.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz, Melanie Bencosme, Nikolay Nikolov, Stephanie Swart, Rafaela Balster and Karl Mollohan

item.title: Apple Reports 17% Sales Jump, Powered by iPhones
item.description: John Ternus, who will take over in September, spoke publicly on an investor call for the first time since his new job was announced.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang
item.media:credit: Lucia Vazquez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Apple’s quarterly results on Thursday topped Wall Street’s expectations.

item.title: Elon Musk suffered a rare loss in his last jury trial.
item.dc:creator: Kate Conger and Natallie Rocha
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: Elon Musk arriving at the Phillip Burton Federal Building and U.S. Courthouse in San Francisco in March.

item.title: Musk says he ‘was a fool’ to provide OpenAI’s early funding.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: Elon Musk’s testimony grew testy during cross-examination from OpenAI’s lawyer

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac

item.title: Palantir Is Making a French Chore Coat. Yes, That Palantir.
item.description: The data analysis company is selling a version of the jacket that it says will show its commitment to “re-industrializing America.”
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi
item.media:credit: Palantir
item.media:description: Palantir’s new chore coat comes in bright blue and black.

item.title: Musk Says He ‘Was a Fool’ to Provide OpenAI’s Early Funding
item.description: In the second day of a trial pitting Mr. Musk against OpenAI, he said the company’s chief executive, Sam Altman, had misled him. But OpenAI’s lawyer said evidence showed the opposite.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: Elon Musk’s testimony grew testy during cross-examination from OpenAI’s lawyer.

item.title: A.I. Spending Sets a Record, With No End in Sight
item.description: Google, Amazon, Microsoft and Meta reported more than $130 billion in quarterly capital expenditures on Wednesday as they build A.I. data centers. There’s more to come.
item.dc:creator: Karen Weise
item.media:credit: AJ Mast for The New York Times
item.media:description: Amazon’s data center complex in New Carlisle, Ind. The company, along with Google, Microsoft and Meta, has spent billions on capital expenditures, largely data centers.

item.title: Ford Reports Higher Profit Thanks Partly to Tariff Refund
item.description: Ford Motor said it expected the federal government to refund $1.3 billion the company paid in tariffs that the Supreme Court later struck down.
item.dc:creator: Neal E. Boudette
item.media:credit: Joe Raedle/Getty Images
item.media:description: Ford Motor vehicles at a dealership in Miami.

item.title: Even without OpenAI, Elon Musk has made A.I. a big part of his business empire.
item.dc:creator: Erin Griffith, Mike Isaac and Ryan Mac
item.media:credit: Mario Tama/Getty Images
item.media:description: SpaceX said a deal with Cursor gave it the option to buy the artificial intelligence start-up, which makes code-writing software, for $60 billion later this year.

item.title: Who is the judge in the case?
item.dc:creator: Erin Griffith and Tripp Mickle
item.media:credit: Drew Angerer/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers of the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California.

item.title: Et tu, Brute? What Elon Musk’s clash with Sam Altman is really about.
item.dc:creator: David Streitfeld
item.media:credit: Saumya Khandelwal for The New York Times
item.media:description: OpenAI is at the center of the clash between Elon Musk and Sam Altman.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac

item.title: A.I. Helps Online Ad Businesses Boom
item.description: Google and Meta are enjoying a digital ad boom, as artificial intelligence automates marketing and drives record sales.
item.dc:creator: Tripp Mickle and Eli Tan
item.media:credit: Shira Inbar

item.title: In Backlash Against Tech in Schools, Parents Are Winning Rollbacks
item.description: From Salt Lake City to New York City, parents are demanding more sway over the digital tools that schools give children.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Singer
item.media:credit: Mark Abramson for The New York Times
item.media:description: Sandra Martinez Roe, with her son, is a leader of a Los Angeles parents’ group pushing the district to audit its technology contracts and limit the use of tech in schools.

item.title: A.I. Bots Told Scientists How to Make Biological Weapons
item.description: Scientists shared transcripts with The Times in which chatbots described how to assemble deadly pathogens and unleash them in public spaces.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel J.X. Dance

item.title: OpenAI Trial Starts With Two Very Different Tales of a Company’s Early Years
item.description: In the trial’s first day of testimony, Elon Musk said greed led co-founder Sam Altman to pull the A.I. lab away from its nonprofit roots. OpenAI says that’s nonsense.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac
item.media:credit: Brennan Smart for The New York Times
item.media:description: Elon Musk arriving at the Ronald V. Dellums U.S. Courthouse in Oakland Tuesday.

item.title: Here’s what OpenAI’s lawyer argued in his opening statement.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz, Mike Isaac and Anushka Patil
item.media:credit: Brennan Smart for The New York Times
item.media:description: William Savitt, OpenAI’s lead counsel, arriving to court on Tuesday.

item.title: Why does Elon Musk talk so much about saving humanity?
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac and Mike Isaac
item.media:credit: Adrees Latif/Reuters
item.media:description: Elon Musk has said that he founded SpaceX, his rocket manufacturer, to take humans to Mars.

item.title: Supreme Court Appears Skeptical of Falun Gong Lawsuit Against Cisco
item.description: The court’s decision could have broader implications for lawsuits seeking to hold companies liable for international human rights abuses.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:credit: Jonah Markowitz for The New York Times
item.media:description: Members of Falun Gong during a protest meditation in Manhattan in 2024. The Chinese government has banned the group.

item.title: Here’s what Musk’s lawyer argued in his opening statement.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz, Mike Isaac and Anushka Patil
item.media:credit: Brennan Smart for The New York Times
item.media:description: Elon Musk arriving to court on Tuesday.

item.title: Who are the lawyers representing Elon Musk, OpenAI and Microsoft?
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz

item.title: Google Signs A.I. Deal With the Pentagon
item.description: The Pentagon has also signed deals for using A.I. on classified networks with OpenAI and Elon Musk’s xAI, amid a dispute with Anthropic.
item.dc:creator: Sheera Frenkel, Tripp Mickle and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Kelsey McClellan for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Mountain View, Calif., campus of Google, which reached an agreement similar to the Pentagon’s deals with OpenAI and xAI.

item.title: Here Are the Key Players in the OpenAI Trial
item.description: Elon Musk, Sam Altman and several other key artificial intelligence industry figures are slated to testify in the trial, which is expected to last several weeks.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz
item.media:credit: Eric Lee/The New York Times; Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times

item.title: This trial comes amid a growing national backlash against A.I.
item.dc:creator: Tripp Mickle
item.media:credit: Luke Sharrett for The New York Times
item.media:description: Anti-data center signage is displayed outside Bart and Amy Snyder’s farm in Wolcott, Ind. The Snyders have filed a lawsuit seeking to stop the planned construction of an A.I. data center next door to their property.

item.title: G.M.’s Profit Bolstered by a $500 Million Tariff Refund
item.description: Because of a February Supreme Court decision, General Motors said it would receive refunds on some of the tariffs it had paid.
item.dc:creator: Neal E. Boudette
item.media:credit: Brett Carlsen for The New York Times
item.media:description: A General Motors plant in Spring Hill, Tenn. The company is still affected by other tariffs — including those on imported steel, aluminum, cars and auto parts.

item.title: Elon Musk Assails Sam Altman on Social Media Before OpenAI Trial
item.description: Mr. Musk’s posts on X have pushed his narrative that OpenAI has lost its way.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Elon Musk on his phone during President Trump’s joint address to Congress at the U.S. Capitol last year.

item.title: What are Elon Musk’s claims against Altman and OpenAI?
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz
item.media:credit: Eric Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: Elon Musk is asking for more than $150 billion in damages from OpenAI and Microsoft, OpenAI’s primary partner.

item.title: Oakland, which is playing host to the trial, is not exactly known as a tech hub.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Ronald V. Dellums Federal Building and U.S. Courthouse in Oakland, Calif.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac

item.title: Here’s the contentious history behind OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: The OpenAI offices in San Francisco.

item.title: Internet Restrictions Spur Russians to Openly Question Putin’s Moves
item.description: From beauty influencers to the token political opposition, Russians are openly questioning President Vladimir V. Putin’s moves to hamstring internet access.
item.dc:creator: Nataliya Vasilyeva and Valerie Hopkins
item.media:credit: Alexander Zemlianichenko/Associated Press
item.media:description: On a subway in Moscow. As Russians criticize the internet restrictions, the discontent has helped send the approval rating of President Vladimir V. Putin to its lowest level since before the war with Ukraine.

item.title: What Elon Musk’s Clash With Sam Altman of OpenAI Is Really About
item.description: Mr. Musk’s lawsuit against Mr. Altman and OpenAI makes the case that all-encompassing greed is Silicon Valley’s defining feature.
item.dc:creator: David Streitfeld
item.media:credit: Illustration by Ben Denzer; Photographs by Eric Lee/The New York Times, Anna Moneymaker/Getty Images

item.title: From Indiana to Idaho, a Backlash Against A.I. Gathers Momentum
item.description: The widening movement is pulling in people from all walks of life, united by a worry that Big Tech will cash in while average Americans bear the costs.
item.dc:creator: Tripp Mickle
item.media:credit: Luke Sharrett for The New York Times
item.media:description: From Indiana to Idaho, a Backlash Against A.I. Gathers Momentum By Tripp Mickle

item.title: Sergey Brin Moves to the Right, With a ‘MAGA Girlfriend’ by His Side
item.description: After once backing liberal causes, the Google co-founder has praised President Trump, donated to Republicans and spent $57 million to try to block a California billionaire tax.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer and Kate Conger
item.media:credit: Brian Snyder/Reuters
item.media:description: Sergey Brin and his girlfriend, Gerelyn “GG” Soto, at a White House artificial-intelligence dinner last year.

item.title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman Bring OpenAI Trial Spectacle to Oakland
item.description: Oakland, Calif., where Elon Musk’s trial against OpenAI and its chief executive, Sam Altman, starts on Monday, is not exactly known as a hub of the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang and Jason Henry
item.media:description: The Ronald V. Dellums Federal Building and U.S. Courthouse in Oakland.

item.title: Microsoft and OpenAI Loosen Their Partnership
item.description: Microsoft, OpenAI’s biggest financial partner, will continue to license the start-up’s technology but will no longer be its exclusive licensee.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Karen Weise
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: OpenAI offices in San Francisco.
